title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products
summary: Aluminum prices fell by up to 2.7% on the London Metal Exchange as traders reacted to reports that President Donald Trump plans to roll back some levies on aluminum and steel products. The Financial Times reported that the move is driven by concerns that the duties are hurting consumers, with aluminum trading at $3,030.00 per ton.

title: US Steel Costs Impacted by War, Trade Deals
summary: Steel prices have continued to rise despite global conflicts, with hot-rolled coil reaching approximately $1,100 per ton. The typical steel pricing upcycle lasts 3–4 months, but the current nine-month upward trend suggests further increases, potentially reaching $1,500 per ton.

title: Steel Market Update: US sheet prices continue to move higher
summary: US sheet steel prices are rising due to extremely limited spot availability, solid demand, and extended lead times. The market remains tight as suppliers struggle to meet growing customer orders.

title: Steel rebar futures fell below CNY 3,070 per ton

title: U.S. Steel Manufacturers Plan Near-Term Capacity Additions
summary: Steel manufacturers across the U.S. are planning over $6 billion in capital-spending projects set to begin in the second half of 2026, signaling near-term capacity growth. These investments aim to strengthen domestic production capabilities amid recovering demand and market stabilization.

title: In the week ending on June 20, 2026, domestic raw steel production was 1,851,000 net tons while the capability utilization rate was 80.2 percent
summary: The American Iron and Steel Institute reported that U.S. raw steel production reached 1,851,000 net tons in the week ending June 20, 2026, with capability utilization at 80.2%, surpassing the administration's 80% target. This indicates a rebound in production efficiency and capacity use following earlier slowdowns.

title: Steel Production in the United States increased to 7500 Thousand Tonnes in May from 7200 Thousand Tonnes in April of 2026
summary: U.S. steel production rose to 7.5 million tonnes in May 2026, up from 7.2 million tonnes in April, according to Trading Economics. The increase reflects a positive trend in domestic output, supported by recovering demand and improved mill utilization rates.

title: Total raw steel produced around the globe rebounded 3% in May to an estimated 157.9 million metric tons
summary: Global raw steel production increased by 3% in May 2026, reaching an estimated 157.9 million metric tons, as reported by the World Steel Association. This rebound suggests a recovery in worldwide steel output amid stabilizing market conditions and rising demand.

title: Copper's role in the energy transition grows as demand surges
summary: Copper companies are ramping up production while embracing sustainability and leveraging digital innovations to address intensifying supply pressures. The sector prioritizes exploration, expansion, and sustainable practices to seize growth opportunities as the energy transition accelerates.

title: How Recycled Copper Plays a Pivotal Role in the Energy Transition
summary: MIT researchers model that recycled copper from end-of-life products could recover around two-thirds of newly available scrap by 2040 if recycling rates double from 32% to 66%. Advanced processing technologies are key to sustainably increasing copper supplies for a clean energy future.

title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
summary: S&P Global projects copper demand will surge 50% from 28 million metric tons in 2025 to 42 million by 2040, driven by AI data centers, renewable energy, and electric vehicles. The study highlights that increased recycling rates could add roughly 6 million metric tons of end-of-life recycled copper by 2040.

title: The role of copper in the energy transition
summary: DNV recommends a four-pronged response to copper scarcity including resource diversification, recycling, and technology innovation to ensure sustainable development goals are met. The report emphasizes that developing technologies to use copper more efficiently is critical to addressing the impending supply-demand imbalance.

title: Copper for Tomorrow: Sustainable copper production
summary: The 'Copper for Tomorrow' initiative aims to solve value chain challenges to increase sustainable copper production globally. This bid focuses on integrating circular economy principles and digital advancements to lead toward a greener future.

title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
summary: Global nickel prices retreated to $17,191.98 per ton on June 23, 2026, marking a 2.12% decline driven by sluggish trading sentiment and structural oversupply concerns. title: Nickel Market Eyes $20,000 as Supply Discipline Meets Surging Demand
source: YouTube (Canada Nickel CEO Mark Selby Interview)
summary: Canada Nickel CEO Mark Selby outlined a constructive outlook for the nickel market in early 2026, supported by Indonesian supply discipline and robust demand fundamentals, with prices settling in a $16,500-$18,500 per ton range. Selby expects nickel to move toward $20,000 per ton within the coming month as production data confirms Indonesia's adherence to flat-to-down ore production targets.

title: Nickel Price Update: Q1 2026 in Review - Investing News Network
summary: Nickel is expected to remain in oversupply through 2026 due to high output from Indonesia and soft demand from manufacturing, with prices trading within a wide range of US$17,000 to US$18,800 per metric ton in Q1. BMI forecasts a 2026 nickel price of US$15,800, while Goldman Sachs is more bullish, anticipating an average of US$17,200 this year.

summary: Nickel prices in May 2026 saw Northeast Asia at USD 21.45/kg (up 13.3%) and Europe at USD 19.58/kg (up 19.5%), following a period of decline in April where Northeast Asia was at 18.93 USD/Kg. title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has notified PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's largest nickel mine, to drastically cut its ore output from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons in 2026 to boost global prices. The company plans to apply for a revision of this quota, while nickel prices recently rose following Indonesia's announcement of potential supply cuts.

title: Nickel price spikes as risks to supply grow in top producer Indonesia
summary: Nickel prices rose 1.3% to $18,806 a ton on the LME after reports that 10% to 15% of high-grade nickel pig iron capacity at Indonesia's Weda Bay Industrial Park will undergo rotational maintenance. This supply risk in the world's top producer has triggered a sharp rally in futures during Asian trading hours.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: Indonesia confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue, while reversing its mining quota policy to regulate supply volatility. The move aims to crack down on environmentally harmful operations and stabilize the market amid actual production lagging behind quotas.

title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
summary: This article investigates how optimizing nickel production can create a sustainable global battery industry by implementing technologies that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and improve energy efficiency. It emphasizes that enhancing stakeholder engagement and robust ESG disclosure standards are essential for transparency in the nickel sector.

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
summary: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials developed a carbon-free, energy-saving method for nickel extraction using hydrogen plasma instead of carbon, cutting CO2 emissions by 84%. This single-step process is up to 18% more energy-efficient and can process low-grade nickel ores, which account for 60% of total reserves.

title: Nickel – a mineral with a challenging role in clean tech
summary: Nickel is crucial for the net-zero transition with demand expected to grow 65% this decade, making it imperative that production scales up with minimal environmental impact. A meta-analysis confirms that clean energy technologies are the fastest-growing sector for nickel demand, requiring the industry to adopt more sustainable practices.

title: Clean energy's nickel rush is heading straight for some of Earth's richest ecosystems
summary: Meeting future nickel demand for clean energy and stainless steel will require tough decisions that may impact priority conservation areas in tropical regions. A study published in Nature Ecology & Evolution highlights that growing nickel supply from the tropics threatens these critical ecosystems.

title: Tenaris acquires control of Italy-based Fast

title: Italy's Prysmian sees stronger U.S. pricing, margins after new copper tariffs
summary: Milan-listed Prysmian, the world's largest cable maker, reported a 2.1% share increase following announcements of stronger U.S. pricing and margins due to new copper tariffs.
